name: Volatile Computer Memory
description: You’ve located a SODIMM RAM stick. Unlike a hard drive, this memory is 'volatile,' meaning it requires a constant flow of electrons to hold onto its data; as soon as the power cuts, every bit of information inside is wiped clean. This specific stick was likely harvested from an old laptop and is currently awaiting a second life in a new build—or a permanent home in this desk's hardware graveyard.
